question: Which Indian city was ranked 6th in the Time Out World's Happiest Cities 2026 Survey?
detail:
- In Time Out's **World's Happiest Cities 2026** survey, **Jaipur** — the capital of Rajasthan — was named the world's **6th happiest city**.
- Jaipur was the **only Indian city** in the Top 10 for 2026. **Bath (England)** took first place.
- **Top 10 Happiest Cities (2026):** 1. Bath (England), 2. Panama City (Panama), 3. Guadalajara (Mexico), 4. Medellín (Colombia), 5. Kraków (Poland), 6. **Jaipur (India)**, 7. Chicago (USA), 8. Cape Town (South Africa), 9. Shanghai (China), 10. Gothenburg (Sweden).
- **About the survey:** Time Out's annual survey ranking the world's happiest cities. Based on **24,000+ global responses**. Parameters — community life, culture, parks & green spaces, food, art, nightlife, social connections and overall happiness.
- **Note:** Time Out had also released a **World's Best Cities for Art and Culture 2026** list, in which Jaipur ranked **18th**.

question: How much financing package has the World Bank approved for PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana?
detail:
- The **World Bank** approved a financing package of **USD 890 million** for the **PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana**.
- The support will help promote household **rooftop solar systems** and take solar power to **10 million (1 crore) homes**.
- **About the scheme:** a Government of India rooftop solar scheme aimed at getting solar panels installed on people's roofs, promoting solar energy in homes and cutting electricity bills. Launched **2024**; target — rooftop solar on **1 crore (10 million)** homes; under the **Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)**.
- **About the World Bank:** an international financial institution that gives loans and financial assistance to developing countries for reducing poverty and for infrastructure, education, health and development projects. Established **1944**; headquarters **Washington, D.C., USA**; President **Ajay Banga**.

question: India and Indonesia have signed an agreement for the supply of the indigenous Astra Mk-1. What is Astra Mk-1?
detail:
- India and Indonesia signed a defence agreement for the supply of the **Astra Mk-1 Beyond Visual Range Air-to-Air Missile (BVRAAM)**.
- Under it India will supply the indigenous Astra Mk-1 to Indonesia, which becomes the **first country to buy** this missile.
- It is an indigenous **air-to-air missile** developed by India, and will boost defence exports and **Atmanirbhar Bharat**.
- **About Astra Mk-1:** India's indigenous BVRAAM, developed to destroy enemy fighter aircraft from long range. Developed by **DRDO**; produced by **Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL)**; speed **Mach 4.5**; range **80–110 km**; operational altitude — sea level to 20 km. It can be fitted on fighters like the **Su-30MKI, Tejas Mk-1A** and **MiG-29**.
- **Note:** Astra Mk-2 has a range of about **200 km**, while **Astra Mk-3 (Gandiva)** is under development with an estimated range of over **350 km**.
- **Indonesia:** capital **Jakarta** (new capital **Nusantara**, under development); President **Prabowo Subianto**.

question: When is World Population Day observed?
detail:
- **World Population Day** is observed every year on **11 July**.
- It was established in **1989** by the **United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)**.
- It has its origin in **11 July 1987**, when the world's population reached about **5 billion**.
- Its aim is to raise awareness of issues like population, maternal health, poverty, employment, migration and access to essential services.
- **Theme 2026:** "Realizing the hopes and aspirations of young people – today and for the future".

question: What rank has the Indian Air Force (IAF) secured in the World Directory of Modern Military Aircraft (WDMMA) Global Air Powers Rankings 2026?
detail:
- In the **WDMMA Global Air Powers Rankings 2026**, the **Indian Air Force (IAF)** was placed **third** among countries.
- **Top 3 air forces:** 1. **USA**, 2. **Russia**, 3. **India**.
- **About the rankings:** released every year by the World Directory of Modern Military Aircraft, assessing the strength of the world's major air forces. The ranking is based not just on the number of aircraft but on **combat capability, modern technology, training, logistics, maintenance and mission preparedness**.
- **IAF fleet:** total aircraft **1,716** — fighter jets **542**, helicopters **498**, transport aircraft **282**, trainers **374**.
- **Note:** WDMMA is **not** a government or UN body — it is an independent global military aviation database that publishes analysis and rankings of modern military aircraft and air forces.

question: Which state will host the 8th Khelo India Youth Games (KIYG) 2026?
detail:
- Union Minister of Youth Affairs and Sports **Shri Mansukh Mandaviya** announced that **Telangana** will host the **8th Khelo India Youth Games (KIYG) 2026**.
- The **7th edition** was held in **Bihar** from **4 to 15 May 2025**, where **Maharashtra** topped the medal table with **158 medals**.
- **7th edition medal table:** 1. Maharashtra — 58 gold, 47 silver, 53 bronze (**158**); 2. Haryana — 39/27/51 (**117**); 3. Rajasthan — 24/12/24 (**60**).
- **About KIYG:** a sports competition for youth under the Khelo India programme, held every year. First held in **2018**. Conducted in **2 categories** — Under-17 school students and Under-21 college students.
- **About Khelo India:** a major Government of India initiative, launched in **2018** in New Delhi with the Khelo India School Games (now Khelo India Youth Games). First editions under it — KIYG **2018**, Khelo India University Games (KIUG) **2020**, Khelo India Winter Games **2020**, Khelo India Para Games **2023**.

question: Indian-origin NASA astronaut Anil Menon is set to travel to which space station?
detail:
- Indian-origin NASA astronaut **Anil Menon** will travel on **14 July 2026** to the **International Space Station (ISS)** aboard **Roscosmos**' (Russia's national space agency) **Soyuz MS-29** spacecraft, from the **Baikonur Cosmodrome** in Kazakhstan.
- This will be his **first space mission**. He will stay on the ISS for about **8 months** under the **Expedition 74** mission.
- During it he will carry out scientific experiments on **microgravity, human health, Artificial Intelligence (AI)** and **semiconductor crystals**.
- **About Anil Menon:** born in **Minneapolis, USA**, to an **Indian–Ukrainian** family. Selected as a NASA astronaut in **2021 (Astronaut Group 23)**. Former roles — **NASA Flight Surgeon** and **SpaceX's first Flight Surgeon**.

question: Where was the 28th meeting of the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) Society held?
detail:
- The **28th meeting** of the **Wildlife Institute of India (WII) Society** was held at the **Central Academy for State Forest Service (CaSFOS)** in **Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu**.
- It was chaired by Union Environment, Forest and Climate Change Minister **Bhupender Yadav**.
- The meeting had special discussion on **wildlife research, human-wildlife conflict, technology-based wildlife management** and capacity building.
- **About WII:** India's leading national institute for wildlife conservation, research and training. Established **1982**; headquarters **Dehradun, Uttarakhand**; administrative ministry — **Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change**.

question: According to the All India Survey on Higher Education (AISHE) Report 2023–24, which state has the highest Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) in Higher Education?
detail:
- According to the **AISHE Report 2023–24**, **Tamil Nadu** ranked first in the country in higher education with a **Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) of 52.3%** — well above the national average of **30%**.
- **What is GER:** it tells you what percentage of all people in the official age group for a level of education are actually studying at that level. For higher education the age group is **18–23 years**. Example: if there are 100 youths aged 18–23 and 52 of them are in college/university, the GER is 52%.
- **Key points from the report:** total higher education enrolment — **4.50 crore** students (the highest ever); **female GER — 31.2%** (higher than males); **STEM enrolment** — over **1.02 crore** students; **women in STEM — 44%**.
- **About AISHE:** an annual report released by the Government of India's **Ministry of Education**, collecting higher education data from all colleges and universities in the country.

question: According to the IUCN Red List 2026, which animal group is most affected by deep-sea mining?
detail:
- According to the **IUCN (International Union for Conservation of Nature) Red List 2026**, **62%** of **Hydrothermal Vent Mollusc** species are at risk of **extinction** because of deep-sea mining.
- The **sediment plumes** thrown up by mining in the ocean depths damage their habitat, making it hard for them to survive.
- **Deep-sea mining** is the process of extracting rare minerals from the sea floor, and is considered a major threat to hydrothermal vent ecosystems.
- **About Hydrothermal Vent Molluscs:** molluscs (soft-bodied sea creatures) that live around hydrothermal vents in the deep sea — snails, mussels, clams and the like.
- **Hydrothermal vents** are natural openings deep in the ocean that release extremely hot water (up to **450°C**), around which special kinds of organisms are found.
